title: Recovery transcript-root runtime-aware (second-runtime path configurable)
action: recovery.mjs hardcodes the Claude projects root (~/.claude/projects via claudeProjectsRoot; Codex sessions resolve to [] per its :221-224 comment). Make the transcript root runtime-aware WITHOUT guessing Codex internals: (1) readConfig gains optional recovery.transcript_roots (array of {runtime, path} entries; absent = today's Claude-only behavior); (2) detectCrashCandidates consults the configured roots in addition to the Claude default, tagging each candidate with its runtime; unreadable/missing configured roots degrade to [] with one console.warn naming the path (never a throw); (3) bee recovery scan --json surfaces the per-root scan result (scanned/skipped+reason) so a Codex user can SEE the root was or wasn't consulted. Docs: one paragraph in docs/specs/workflow-state.md B33 section noting the config key. Tests: test_misc.mjs (or the recovery rows' current home) — configured extra root with a fabricated transcript is scanned and tagged; missing configured root degrades with warn; no config = byte-identical. RED first, then GREEN + full verify.

truths: recovery.transcript_roots config consulted; candidates tagged with runtime; missing/unreadable roots degrade gracefully and VISIBLY (per-root scan result in --json).
No config key = byte-identical behavior to today.
prohibitions: Do NOT hardcode any Codex-internal path guess as a default — configuration only.
Do NOT change the mining-window or prompt-building logic.

trace.outcome: recovery.mjs's transcript-root detection is now runtime-aware: .bee/config.json recovery.transcript_roots lets a second runtime (e.g. Codex) name its own transcript store; detectCrashCandidates consults it alongside the Claude default and tags each candidate with the runtime that found it, missing/unreadable configured roots degrade to skipped+reason with one console.warn (never a throw), and both bee recovery scan and bee status --json surface every root's scan result. No config key is byte-identical to before.
trace.verify_output: PASS run_verify: 53 suite(s), concurrency=5, wall=34158ms (rerun: test_render_race showed the known WSL2 flake on 2nd pass, PASS on 1st and 3rd pass)
